title: Maintenance, Commit Graphs, Index Acceleration, and Bundles · History Defense
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for maintenance, commit graphs, index acceleration, and bundles and requires the smallest safe measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.
Configure incremental-repack, loose-objects, commit-graph, prefetch, gc, worktree, rerere-gc, and scheduler strategy from measured need.
Explain commit-graph generations, changed-path Bloom filters, split chains, multi-pack indexes, bitmaps, and compatibility or invalidation boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Case G76 is a quiz in a disposable repository. measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning. The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair. Competency probe: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair. Competency probe: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: git gc only compresses reachable data and can never expire recovery objects or race another process.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph for the quiz Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety case
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology for the quiz Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety case
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation for the quiz Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety case
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path for the quiz Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety case
